item.title: backlog history output missing title column — makes history hard to read
item.intent: Ensure backlog history output includes the item title so users can read the history without cross-referencing backlog list.
item.description: cmd_backlog_history outputs a table with columns: ID, DATE, STATUS, DURATION. It does not include the item title. The archive (sprints.jsonl) does not store the title — it only stores itemId, date, status, duration, filesChanged, linesAdded, linesRemoved, priority, category. To show a title, the command would need to look it up in the active backlog. However, since items may have been cleaned out, the title may not always be available. The fix should: (1) attempt to look up the title from active backlogs, and (2) fall back to showing the itemId if the item has been cleaned. This makes history scannable without requiring a separate backlog list lookup.
steps: In cmd_backlog_history(), after collecting the jq results, join each itemId against the active backlogs to retrieve the title
Build a lookup map of id->title from both backlog.json and bugs.json using jq before printing the table
Add a TITLE column to the printf format string in the table header and the formatted output
When a title is not found in active backlogs (item was cleaned), fall back to displaying the itemId itself or '-' in the title column
Adjust column widths: ID 12, DATE 12, STATUS 10, DURATION 10, TITLE remainder
Test: run backlog history on a repo with both active and cleaned items to verify titles are shown correctly
acceptanceCriteria: backlog history output includes a TITLE column showing the item's title from the active backlog
When an item has been cleaned from the backlog, history shows '-' or the itemId in the title column rather than failing
Column headers are aligned: ID, DATE, STATUS, DURATION, TITLE
Existing --since, --failed, --limit flags still work correctly with the updated output
